title: quercus × cantabrica
intro: Quercus × cantabrica, also known as the Cantabrian oak, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Quercus robur and Quercus faginea, two species of oak trees native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Quercus × cantabrica include its ability to soothe digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and even help lower cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
